title: Microsoft Outlook Message
description: JSON Schema for a Microsoft Graph message resource representing an email message in a user's mailbox. Based on the microsoft.graph.message resource type from the Microsoft Graph v1.0 API.

id.description: Unique identifier for the message. By default, this value changes when the item is moved from one container to another. Use the Prefer: IdType="ImmutableId" header for immutable identifiers.

createdDateTime.description: The date and time the message was created. Uses ISO 8601 format in UTC time (e.g., 2014-01-01T00:00:00Z).

lastModifiedDateTime.description: The date and time the message was last changed. Uses ISO 8601 format in UTC time.

categories.description: The categories associated with the message.

receivedDateTime.description: The date and time the message was received. Uses ISO 8601 format in UTC time.

sentDateTime.description: The date and time the message was sent. Uses ISO 8601 format in UTC time.

hasAttachments.description: Indicates whether the message has attachments. This property does not include inline attachments. To verify inline attachments, parse the body property for a src attribute.

internetMessageId.description: The message ID in the format specified by RFC 2822.

internetMessageHeaders.description: A collection of message headers defined by RFC 5322. Includes custom message headers starting with 'x-'. Returned only on applying a $select query option.

body.description: The body of the message. Can be in HTML or text format.

bodyPreview.description: The first 255 characters of the message body in text format.

parentFolderId.description: The unique identifier for the message's parent mailFolder.

conversationId.description: The ID of the conversation the email belongs to.

conversationIndex.description: Indicates the position of the message within the conversation. Binary value encoded as base64.

isDeliveryReceiptRequested.description: Indicates whether a delivery receipt is requested for the message.

isReadReceiptRequested.description: Indicates whether a read receipt is requested for the message.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the message has been read.

isDraft.description: Indicates whether the message is a draft. A message is a draft if it has not been sent yet.

webLink.description: The URL to open the message in Outlook on the web. Append ispopout=0 to show in the review pane instead of a popout window.

inferenceClassification.description: The classification of the message for the user, based on inferred relevance or importance, or on an explicit override.

flag.description: The follow-up flag value indicating the status, start date, due date, or completion date for the message.

from.description: The owner of the mailbox from which the message is sent. In most cases, this is the same as the sender property, except for sharing or delegation scenarios.

sender.description: The account that is used to generate the message. In most cases, this is the same as the from property. Can differ when sending from a shared mailbox or as a delegate.

toRecipients.description: The To: recipients for the message.

ccRecipients.description: The Cc: recipients for the message.

bccRecipients.description: The Bcc: recipients for the message.

replyTo.description: The email addresses to use when replying.

uniqueBody.description: The part of the body of the message that is unique to the current message. Not returned by default; use $select=uniqueBody.

attachments.description: The fileAttachment and itemAttachment attachments for the message.

Recipient.title: Recipient
Recipient.description: Represents information about a user in the context of a message (sender, from, to, cc, bcc, replyTo).

EmailAddress.title: Email Address
EmailAddress.description: Represents the name and email address of a person or entity.

name.description: The display name of the person or entity.

address.description: The email address of the person or entity.

ItemBody.title: Item Body
ItemBody.description: Represents the body content of a message, event, or other item.

contentType.description: The type of the content. Possible values are text and html.

content.description: The content of the item body.

InternetMessageHeader.title: Internet Message Header
InternetMessageHeader.description: A key-value pair representing an Internet message header as defined by RFC 5322. Custom headers should start with 'x-'.

name.description: The name of the message header.

value.description: The value for the message header.

FollowupFlag.title: Follow-up Flag
FollowupFlag.description: Represents the follow-up flag status, start date, due date, or completion date for a message.

flagStatus.description: The follow-up status for the message.

startDateTime.description: The date and time to start the follow up.

dueDateTime.description: The date and time the follow up is due.

completedDateTime.description: The date and time the follow up was completed.

DateTimeTimeZone.title: DateTime with Time Zone
DateTimeTimeZone.description: Describes the date, time, and time zone of a point in time.

dateTime.description: A single point of time in a combined date and time representation (e.g., 2017-08-29T04:00:00.0000000).

timeZone.description: Represents a time zone (e.g., Pacific Standard Time). See supported time zones.

Attachment.title: Attachment
Attachment.description: Base type for file, item, and reference attachments on a message. Use @odata.type to distinguish between types.

@odata.type.description: The OData type discriminator for the attachment.

id.description: The unique identifier of the attachment.

name.description: The display name of the attachment. Does not need to be the actual file name.

contentType.description: The MIME type of the attachment.

size.description: The length of the attachment in bytes.

isInline.description: True if the attachment is an inline attachment; otherwise, false.

lastModifiedDateTime.description: The date and time the attachment was last modified in UTC (ISO 8601).

contentBytes.description: The base64-encoded contents of the file. Only for fileAttachment type.

contentId.description: The ID of the attachment in the Exchange store. Only for fileAttachment type.

contentLocation.description: The URI corresponding to the location of the content of the attachment.

MailFolder.title: Mail Folder
MailFolder.description: A mail folder in a user's mailbox, such as Inbox and Drafts. Mail folders can contain messages, other Outlook items, and child mail folders.

id.description: The unique identifier of the mail folder.

parentFolderId.description: The unique identifier for the parent mail folder.

childFolderCount.description: The number of immediate child mail folders.

unreadItemCount.description: The number of items marked as unread in the folder.

totalItemCount.description: The number of items in the folder.

isHidden.description: Indicates whether the mail folder is hidden. Can only be set when creating the folder.
